text:
ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE

ATTENDING: [Oncology]
PATIENT: Diane Castellano
DOB/AGE: 55 yo female
DX: Stage IV melanoma, BRAF V600E+

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Routine oncology follow-up. Patient returns for Opdivo (nivolumab) maintenance, cycle 2 assessment. Approximately 13 months from initial diagnosis of stage IV melanoma.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Castellano is a 55-year-old female landscape architect from the southwestern US with a known history of stage IV nodular melanoma, BRAF V600E-mutated (TMB 18.4 mut/Mb), who presents today for her cycle 2 nivolumab maintenance follow-up visit.

To briefly summarize her history: she was initially diagnosed with stage IV disease and started on dabrafenib/trametinib (dab/tram), achieving a partial response by 3 months with disease stabilization at 6 months. At approximately 8 months from diagnosis, imaging demonstrated progression with new intracranial metastases. She underwent stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) to the brain lesions followed by ipilimumab/nivolumab (ipi/nivo) combination therapy. Her course was complicated by grade 3 immune-mediated colitis after cycle 1, requiring hospitalization and high-dose corticosteroids (IV methylprednisolone, then oral prednisone taper). She was subsequently transitioned to nivolumab (Opdivo) 480 mg q4w maintenance monotherapy following the colitis event and resolution of steroid taper, with imaging at approximately 11 months showing early mixed/evolving response.

She has now completed 2 cycles of Opdivo maintenance (480 mg IV q4w). Today she reports feeling considerably better compared to her hospitalization period. She notes mild fatigue, which she describes as roughly baseline — manageable, not limiting her daily activities. She is working part-time from home on landscape projects. She denies any recurrence of diarrhea, abdominal cramping, or rectal urgency. No hematochezia. No nausea or vomiting. Appetite is improved. She does note some mild weight gain (~2 lbs) since last visit, which she attributes to improved appetite.

She reports mild cold intolerance over the past 3-4 weeks — she has been feeling more chilly than usual, particularly in the mornings. She also notes some mild constipation (BMs every 2-3 days, no straining, no blood) and slightly dry skin. She initially attributed these symptoms to the desert climate and dehydration but reports they have been persistent. She denies neck pain or swelling, dysphagia, or palpitations. No polyuria, polydipsia, or significant weight loss. No new skin lesions noted by patient. No headaches, visual changes, or focal neurological symptoms.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
(+) Mild fatigue, grade 1 per patient report
(+) Mild cold intolerance
(+) Mild constipation
(+) Dry skin
(-) Diarrhea, abdominal pain, rectal bleeding (colitis resolved)
(-) Nausea, vomiting, dysphagia
(-) Headache, focal neuro deficits, seizures
(-) Rash, pruritus, mucositis
(-) Dyspnea, cough, chest pain
(-) Arthralgias or myalgias
(-) Dysuria, hematuria
(-) New skin lesions

---

PHYSICAL EXAM:
Vitals: BP 118/74, HR 68, RR 16, Temp 37.0C, O2 sat 98% RA, Wt 63.2 kg (up ~1 kg from last visit)
ECOG PS: 1

General: Alert and oriented x3, well-appearing, in no acute distress. Mood improved compared to prior visits.
HEENT: Anicteric sclerae. Oropharynx clear, no mucositis. No periorbital edema. Thyroid: non-tender, no palpable goiter, no nodules appreciated on exam. No cervical lymphadenopathy.
Skin: No new pigmented lesions. No rash. Skin mildly dry on bilateral forearms. Prior SRS scalp site well-healed.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. No peripheral edema.
Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly appreciated. Bowel sounds present. No guarding or rebound.
Musculoskeletal: No joint effusions. Full ROM of upper and lower extremities.
Neurological: CN II-XII grossly intact. No focal motor or sensory deficits. Gait steady.
Lymph nodes: No axillary, inguinal, or supraclavicular adenopathy palpated.

---

LAB / TEST RESULTS:
CBC: WBC 6.1, Hgb 12.4 (mild, stable), Plt 198 — no significant change from prior
CMP: Na 138, K 4.0, Cr 0.8, BUN 14, total bili 0.6, ALT 22, AST 19, alk phos 78 — LFTs within normal limits
TSH: 8.2 mIU/L (ref 0.4-4.0) — ELEVATED
Free T4: 0.8 ng/dL (ref 0.8-1.8) — low-normal
Free T3: not ordered today; deferred
TPO antibodies: pending
Cortisol (AM): not ordered at this visit; will check if clinical picture evolves
Lipid panel: deferred to PCP

Note: Prior TSH at ~11 months (last check): 2.9 mIU/L — within normal limits. Current elevation is new.

---

IMAGING:
Most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (approximately 11 months): hepatic lesions present but early evidence of response vs. pseudoprogression following transition to ipi/nivo then Opdivo maintenance. Brain MRI at approximately 8 months: intracranial metastases treated with SRS. No updated imaging today — next CT/MRI planned per surveillance schedule.

---

ASSESSMENT:
1. Stage IV melanoma, BRAF V600E+, on Opdivo (nivolumab) 480 mg q4w maintenance, cycle 2 complete. Clinical trajectory consistent with response — tolerating therapy well from an oncologic standpoint. Fatigue mild and stable at grade 1, not limiting function.

2. New immune-mediated hypothyroidism, grade 1 — almost certainly irAE from nivolumab (or possibly late effect of prior ipi/nivo). TSH now 8.2 with low-normal free T4. Mild symptoms (cold intolerance, dry skin, constipation) are concordant. No features to suggest adrenal insufficiency or hypophysitis at this time (hemodynamics stable, morning cortisol not yet checked but low clinical suspicion). This is the most common endocrine irAE with checkpoint inhibitor therapy and per NCCN/ASCO guidelines does not require treatment interruption at grade 1.

3. History of grade 3 immune-mediated colitis — resolved. GI symptoms have NOT recurred. Stable.

4. Brain metastases s/p SRS — neurologically intact. No new symptoms. Will image per schedule.

---

PLAN:
1. Opdivo (nivolumab) 480 mg IV q4w — CONTINUE. Cycle 3 to be infused today per schedule. No dose modification warranted. Will continue to monitor for irAEs at each visit. Given prior grade 3 colitis, patient counseled to report any GI symptoms immediately.

2. Immune-mediated hypothyroidism, grade 1:
   - Initiating levothyroxine 50 mcg PO daily. Counseled to take on empty stomach, 30-60 minutes before first meal or coffee.
   - Recheck TSH and free T4 in 6-8 weeks. Will uptitrate as needed.
   - Endocrinology referral placed; given low-grade nature, may manage in oncology if TSH trending toward goal.
   - TPO antibodies pending — results to be reviewed at next visit.
   - No Opdivo hold indicated for grade 1 hypothyroidism.
   - Patient verbalized understanding of new diagnosis and medication.

3. Fatigue, grade 1 — reassured. Likely multifactorial (mild hypothyroidism, post-treatment recovery, mild anemia). Expect improvement with levothyroxine. Counseled on sleep hygiene, gentle activity as tolerated.

4. Imaging — repeat CT C/A/P and brain MRI to be ordered for approximately 15 months from diagnosis (next scheduled imaging window). Will review at follow-up.

5. Labs prior to cycle 3 (today): CBC, CMP, TSH — reviewed above. LFTs within normal range, cleared for infusion.

6. Follow-up: Return in 4 weeks for Opdivo cycle 3 and labs. Will also review TPO antibody result and levothyroxine tolerance at that time.

Patient has been informed of the plan. She expressed understanding and has no further questions at this time. She is motivated and engaged in her care. Overall doing well — encouraged by trajectory.

---

Electronically signed,
[Oncology Attending]
[Institution Oncology Clinic]
